    • toInitiateMultipartUploadRequest

      public com.aliyun.sdk.service.oss2.models.InitiateMultipartUploadRequest toInitiateMultipartUploadRequest(MultipartUploadRequest request)

    • rejectUnsupportedChecksum

      public static void rejectUnsupportedChecksum(ChecksumMethod algorithm)
      OSS computes a CRC64-ECMA checksum over uploaded objects and exposes no CRC32C or SHA256 object checksum. A null algorithm means "use the substrate-native default" (CRC64 on OSS) and is allowed; any other explicit algorithm is rejected up front so callers receive a clear failure rather than a checksum value labeled with an algorithm OSS did not produce.

    • toAliRetryer

      public static com.aliyun.sdk.service.oss2.retry.Retryer toAliRetryer(RetryConfig config)
      Converts a cloud-agnostic RetryConfig to an Ali OSS v2 SDK Retryer.

      Mapping:

      • EXPONENTIAL mode → EqualJitterBackoff with baseDelay and maxBackoff
      • FIXED mode → FixedDelayBackoff with constant delay
      • maxAttempts → StandardRetryer.Builder.maxAttempts(Integer)

      Note: The Ali OSS v2 SDK does not support a totalTimeout equivalent (a hard deadline across all retry attempts combined). This field from RetryConfig is not applied. The attemptTimeout is handled separately via readWriteTimeout on the client builder. The multiplier field is not directly configurable — EqualJitterBackoff uses 2x internally.

    • toHttpClientOptions

      public static com.aliyun.sdk.service.oss2.transport.HttpClientOptions toHttpClientOptions(String proxyHost, Duration readWriteTimeout, Integer maxConnections, Duration idleConnectionTimeout)
      Builds HttpClientOptions carrying the transport settings MultiCloudJ exposes that map onto the OSS SDK's HTTP client. Only non-null inputs are applied; any option left unset keeps the SDK's own default, so a client built from these options behaves identically to the SDK default client for the unset fields.

      The OSS SDK's OSSClient/OSSAsyncClient builders expose no connection-pool or idle-timeout setters directly; those live only on HttpClientOptions. Callers therefore build a transport client (sync Apache5HttpClientBuilder or async Apache5AsyncHttpClientBuilder) from these options and supply it via .httpClient(...). Because supplying a client bypasses the SDK's own toHttpClientOptions derivation, the two transport fields the driver otherwise sets on the client builder (proxyHost, readWriteTimeout) must be passed in here so they are preserved. Reconciled against alibabacloud-oss-v2:0.4.0.

      Parameters:
      proxyHost - host:port proxy string, or null to leave unset
      readWriteTimeout - resolved read/write timeout, or null to leave unset
      maxConnections - max connection-pool size, or null to leave unset
      idleConnectionTimeout - idle-connection (keep-alive) timeout, or null to leave unset
      Returns:
      the assembled HttpClientOptions
